title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's spreads — Health Edge Daily | Jul 08
description: Researchers have just made the groundbreaking discovery that a common brain protein may be responsible for spreading Alzheimer's disease throughout the brain, carrying toxic Tau proteins from damaged neurons into healthy ones. This finding is monumental, as it reveals a potential new target for therapy, and by blocking these harmful protein packages before they reach new cells, it may one day be possible to slow the disease's relentless progression. title: Millions may be getting the wrong cholesterol test — Health Edge Daily | Jul 06
description: Twenty-five million Americans may be receiving the wrong cholesterol test, a new study suggests, which could lead to unnecessary treatment or, worse, heart attacks and strokes that could have been prevented. The research, published in a recent health journal, reveals that a blood test measuring apoB, a type of harmful cholesterol particle, is better than the standard LDL cholesterol test for determining who needs more intensive treatment. title: Health Edge Daily | Jul 04 — Scientists discover a surprising link between vita
description: Imagine a world where something as simple as a daily vitamin could be the key to a sharper mind and a healthier brain as we age. New research out of Japan suggests that vitamin C may play a crucial role in supporting a healthy aging brain. In a study of over 2,000 older adults, scientists found that those with lower levels of vitamin C in their blood tended to have less gray matter and weaker connections in a key brain network involved in memory, attention, and other cognitive functions. title: Health News Jun 29 — Osteopenia is silently weakening bones in mil
description: You're just one brittle bone away from a potentially life-altering fracture, and millions of people are living with this reality due to a condition known as osteopenia. It's a silent threat that's often overlooked, causing bones to become less dense and more fragile over time. The scary part is that many people only discover they have osteopenia after a fracture or a bone scan, which is why it's crucial to understand the risk factors. title: Health News Jun 29 — Osteopenia is silently weakening bones in mil
description: Breaking news in the world of health: millions of people are silently developing a condition that's weakening their bones, and it's not just about osteoporosis. Osteopenia is a common but often overlooked condition that causes bones to become less dense and more fragile, and because it develops silently, many people only discover they have it after a fracture or bone scan.
